summary_one_line: Input must be a 64-bit ELF whose header e_shnum/e_shoff fields yield section_count() > 1 so the loop guard's true side fires

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid 64-bit ELF header (magic 7f 45 4c 46, ELFCLASS64 = 0x02 at offset 0x04) whose section-header-table fields (e_shoff at 0x28 and e_shnum near 0x3c) parse to a section_count() strictly greater than 1, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() takes its true side and the body at lines 1194-1239 executes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds set offset 0x04 to 0x02 (ELFCLASS64) and carry non-trivial header offset/count fields, so ElfFile::section_count() returns ~286000 hits in W vs ~230 in L and the blocker line reports T=16 in W versus T=0 in L; the loop body lines 1194-1239 are W-only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds use offset 0x04 = 0x01 (ELFCLASS32) with mostly-zeroed or 0x20-padded section-table fields, so section_count() resolves to 0/1, the guard is always false (L1193 shows T=0 F=20), the loop body never runs, and ElfFile::section_count() is hit only ~230 times in L.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other, letting it splice the precise section-count/offset comparand values into the header so the loop guard comparison passes; naive has only blind havoc and never solves the multi-field header constraint.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a loop guard comparing the index against the ELF section count, with the true side globally blocked.
Winner seeds set offset 0x04 to 0x02 (ELFCLASS64) while loser seeds use 0x01 (ELFCLASS32).
The loop body executes only in the winner role; the body lines 1194-1239 are marked [W].
section_count() is invoked ~286000 times in the winner versus ~230 in the loser, showing the count is non-zero only for winners.
The blocker line takes its true side in the winner (T=16) but never in the loser (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1193  T=16 F=12  T=0 F=20  for (Elf64_Xword i = 1; i < elf.section_count(); i++) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability, only the havoc/token mutator stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 87 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed ac3ba2cbb00192e0`; the ancestor chain has 18 levels and the trailing I2S-floor signal reports 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 16 and 17, the lower-bound signature of unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ELF-header harness where cmplog fails to resolve the section_count loop guard no faster than naive (no significant cmplog>naive divergence), indicating I2S substitution does not help solve the multi-field section-table comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact offsets that feed section_count() (e_shoff/e_shnum) are inferred from ELF layout plus diffuse PARTIAL byte-diff signal rather than a single concentrated I2S-substituted offset, so the precise comparand splice point is uncertain.
